title: ugetregf
text: ugetregf Get the double-precision value of a Uniface general variable ($1 ... $99). double ugetregf (shortnum); Copy Parameters num Number of the Uniface general variable. Allowed values are 1 through 99. Return Values The double-precision, floating-point value of the specified general variable. If the value of num is out of range, this function returns 0. However, note that this is also the return value when num is within the range and the general variable has the value 0. Use Library: yrtl Description ugetreg returns the double-precision, floating-point value of the specified Uniface general variable (historically also known as a ‘dollar register’), or 0 (zero) if the specified register does not exist. If the general variable does not contain a numeric value, the return value is zero. The following code fragment retrieves the double-precision floating-point value of general register $10 : Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> double value = ugetregf(10); Copy Related Links ugetreg Get the integer value of a Uniface general variable ($1 ... $99). ugetregs Get the string value of a Uniface general variable ($1 ... $99). uputregf Copy a double-precision floating-point value into the specified general variable ($1 ... $99).
